item.title: M0-tauri-shell: Tauri 2.0 macOS app scaffold
item.intent: cargo tauri build produces a real .app bundle on macOS that opens a window with a webview. The build must succeed in CI without manual intervention. em-core is wired as a direct Rust dependency of the Tauri backend — no separate process, no mocks.
item.description: Scaffold a Tauri 2.0 project targeting macOS. The Tauri backend (src-tauri/) must depend on em-core from the workspace. The webview loads an HTML page (can be a blank page with a placeholder — the editor comes in M0-editor). The app must produce a working window that opens on launch. Wire CI (GitHub Actions) to build the Tauri app on macOS and verify the build artifact exists. This item does NOT include the editor, IPC bridge, or file operations — those come in subsequent M0 items.
steps: Run npm create tauri-app@latest or cargo tauri init in the repo root to scaffold the Tauri 2.0 project
Update the workspace Cargo.toml to include src-tauri as a member
Add em-core as a path dependency in src-tauri/Cargo.toml: em-core = { path = '../em-core' }
Verify the Tauri app compiles: cargo build -p em-markdown (or whatever the tauri crate is named)
Create a minimal src/index.html (or equivalent) — a div with 'Easy Markdown' text is sufficient for M0
Add a GitHub Actions workflow .github/workflows/build.yml that runs on push/PR, checks out code, installs Rust + Node deps, runs cargo tauri build on macOS runner
Confirm the workflow passes and produces a .app artifact

acceptanceCriteria.text: cargo build --workspace completes successfully with src-tauri included and exits 0
em-core is a dependency of the Tauri backend crate — proven by cargo tree
The GitHub Actions build workflow exists, is valid YAML, and has at least one job
acceptanceCriteria.verify: test -f .github/workflows/build.yml && yq e '(.jobs | length) >= 1' .github/workflows/build.yml | grep -q true

acceptanceCriteria.text: Tauri app builds a real bundle on the local machine
